Come near before the Lord— This has been supposed to refer to some particular place, where the Lord manifested his presence. The great tabernacle was not yet built, but there appears to have been a small tabernacle or tent called the Tabernacle of the Congregation, which, after the sin of the golden calf, was always placed without the camp; see Exodus 33:7: And Moses took the Tabernacle and…

Moses stands preeminent above all Israel's prophets. "With him will I speak mouth to mouth, even apparently, and not in dark speeches," the Lord said, "and the similitude of the Lord shall he behold." (Num. 12:8.) Hence we read: "And the Lord spake unto Moses face to face, as a man speaketh unto his friend." (Ex. 33:11.) And: "Moses stood in the presence of God, and talked with him face to face."…

Secondly, this phrase, ‘speaking with the Lord,’ is reminiscent of Exodus 33:11 in which the Lord speaks to Moses ‘face to face, as a man speaketh unto his friend.’ The brother of Jared also had a similar experience. Thus we see that the Book of Mormon confirms the Biblical truth that God is a personage, with a form like man’s although more glorious, and that God usually speaks to prophets ‘as a…

AND MOSES SAID UNTO THE ETERNAL: ‘SEE, THOU SAYEST UNTO ME: BRING UP THIS PEOPLE.’ This happened on Mount Sinai, when Moses went up there on the nineteenth of Tammuz. It was not necessary though for Scripture to say, “and Moses went up to G-d, and he said to him, ‘See, Thou sayest unto me, etc.,’” for it is known that as long as the Glory dwelled upon Mount Sinai, all communications [to Moses]…

Moses is saying to Jehovah: You want me to bring up this people to the promised land, yet you told me you won't go with me. You claim to know me and you know my name. That means you know me. You know that I can't do it alone. Then you also say that I have found grace in thy sight. Now Moses starts to define his own form of GRACE FROM THE LORD. 13. Now therefore, I pray thee, if I have found grace…
